arguments.task: Research Magento (Adobe Commerce) using official and first-party sources where possible. Open developer.adobe.com/commerce and find pages that document API types (REST, GraphQL), authentication methods, rate limits, and access requirements. Also find official Adobe Commerce pricing or product/access pages indicating whether usage is self-serve or contact-sales/paid. Then search for first-party pages or official GitHub pages about an MCP server for Adobe Commerce/Magento. Return a concise evidence-backed summary with exact URLs and quoted passages or clear extracted statements for each claim.

input.thought: Start a browser research run on official Adobe Commerce sources plus official GitHub/MCP evidence.

arguments.task: Use direct URLs and web search if needed. Visit official Adobe Commerce docs/pages to verify: 1) Web API authentication page, 2) REST API docs page, 3) GraphQL docs page, 4) rate limiting page, 5) Adobe Commerce pricing or plans page, 6) any official GitHub/Adobe page for MCP server. Extract exact URLs and short supporting statements only. Avoid looping. If a direct page 404s, search the official adobe.com or developer.adobe.com site for the exact topic and open the best result.

raw_result.description: Enterprise ecommerce platform with APIs for storefront, catalog, checkout, orders, and admin operations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Adobe Commerce developer docs expose Web APIs including REST and GraphQL, and the navigation/evidence gathered references a Web API authentication page. Adobe Commerce commonly uses integration/admin/customer tokens, but the exact officially documented auth taxonomy was not successfully extracted from the page content in this run, so this is marked for review.
raw_result.gating_reason: Evidence gathered shows Adobe Commerce developer resources are public, but Adobe Commerce itself is an enterprise product and no self-serve signup or free/trial access page was verified in this run from first-party pricing/access documentation. Treat as contact-sales/enterprise-gated pending human confirmation.

api_surface.breadth: Official developer documentation includes Web APIs with both REST and GraphQL surfaces for core commerce capabilities, but exact endpoint breadth was not enumerated from extracted page text in this run.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Adobe/Magento MCP server page was verified from the gathered evidence. Marked false with human review due to lack of confirmed official MCP evidence.

raw_result.blocker: Public developer docs appear to exist, but buildability is limited by unverified exact auth details and likely enterprise/product-access gating for obtaining a usable Adobe Commerce instance today.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No official rate-limit documentation was successfully extracted during this run; needs human review.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official Adobe developer hub for Commerce is publicly available; browser evidence indicates dedicated REST and GraphQL documentation paths under the Adobe Commerce developer site.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Several required fields remain only partially verified from the gathered evidence: exact auth methods, official access/pricing tier, rate limits, and whether an official MCP exists. The browser run confirmed relevant official documentation paths but did not extract stable page content for all claims.
